name: claude-session-sync
description: Sync Claude Code conversation history (and skills/MCP) across machines — via an existing file-sync folder (Syncthing/iCloud/Dropbox/OneDrive/Google Drive) OR self-contained over a git remote (no external sync app needed). Per-project locking prevents simultaneous-access corruption, and an in-use (アクセス中) tab lets you safely disconnect a stale lock from any device (blocked while that conversation is actually running). Auto-titles conversations by content and language, and on resume (cc / claude -h) applies that Japanese title to Claude Code's native display name too (--name for the prompt box / resume picker, and the --remote-control name shown on phone/claude.ai) so resumed sessions aren't labelled with English auto-names. On launch it can also auto-read a folder you choose (e.g. an Obsidian vault): c/cfp/cp/cc/ch each toggle this, the first message asks Claude to read the folder's structure and key notes, and you pick auto-send or confirm-each-time (Enter=send / ↓=don't) — all in claude -a / /css. Resume (cc / claude -h) can also run /compact first and open the conversation only after compaction fully completes, so long histories resume already-compressed (per-method toggle in claude -a / /css). Resuming from the claude -h history browser now opens in the working folder that conversation was last in (its own last cwd, read from the transcript) instead of wherever you ran the browser; a Tab action-menu entry lets you resume in the browser's launch folder instead. Detects device switches and tells Claude the right working path. A Knowledge Archive (claude -a) forces Claude to record the knowledge your conversations produce — memory edits, plans, rules, custom concepts, research, context/decisions, images, summaries — into Obsidian (frontmatter + wikilinks + MOC), a local folder, and/or Notion (MCP), each category at a priority you choose (force/duty/optional/off), via a SessionStart context-injection hook. The Archive's aggregation/index (MOC) files are configurable per category so they never clobber an Obsidian vault that already has structure — auto-create a per-folder _index.md, point to an existing file you choose (links are appended into your file, no new _index.md), or turn it off — set in claude -a (with a native file picker) and toggled globally with /css archive moc. The claude -h browser adds favorites, conversation forking, start-new-with-context, resume-with-a-chosen-permission, an in-use tab with remote disconnect, and a separate subagent-history tab; every row is tagged [メイン]/[サブ] so main vs subagent is clear in any tab and terminal. In-use recognition is hardened with a per-prompt heartbeat and stale-lock take-over so an active session is reliably shown as in use. claude -h / claude -a work from any shell — PowerShell, cmd.exe and Git Bash on Windows, bash/zsh on macOS/Linux — via PATH shims, so they no longer fall back to the raw help text outside PowerShell. A check-deps doctor verifies required tools (claude CLI, PowerShell/python3) at install and first run. Short launch commands work in every shell — c (launch), cfp (launch in a fixed folder you pick via a native folder dialog), ch (history), ca (settings) — and a per-launch-method remote-control toggle re-enables phone control on launch. An interactive 'claude -a' settings menu sets up auto-starting claude at login — multiple conversations, each with its own model, thinking depth and permission (plan…full-bypass, with warnings) — plus a multi-instance check, sync status, toggles, and restore-to-original. An in-conversation /css command shows a CLI-style status panel and toggles settings (archive/remote/language) without leaving the chat; stop-required ops are blocked with a notice, and /css gui opens the real settings GUI in a separate window. Windows/macOS/Linux.
